summary.intent: Fix the failing ars-components branch coverage ratchet on main (89.8% vs 90% threshold), then tackle Nightly CI #31 mutation failures across multiple shards, and extend mutation coverage to newly merged agnostic input components after rebasing.
summary.outcome: Branch coverage lifted from 89.8% to 91.8% with 0 missed mutants across all targeted files (HoverCard, Table, DownloadTrigger, Highlight, PasswordInput, SearchInput, NumberInput, PinInput, ars-core SharedState, ars-collections Key); development docs updated to require mutation testing and spec-conformance tests for every new agnostic component; all verification gates (fmt, clippy, xci-fast) passed and changes committed.

repo: Coverage gate is dual-threshold: both lines AND branches must be ≥90%, enforced via `cargo xtask coverage check --file ... --package ars-components --min 90 --branch-min 90`.
`cargo xci-fast` is the required pre-push gate and runs 10 sequential stages: fmt, check, clippy, unit, integration, adapter, spec snippets, error variant coverage, mutual exclusion, snapshot count.
Spec-conformance tests live in `crates/ars-components/tests/spec_conformance/` and proptest state machines in `crates/ars-components/tests/proptest_state_machines/`.

Equivalent mutant exclusions are documented in `.cargo/mutants.toml` with function-name-based regex patterns; skip regex must include trailing `=` to match cargo-mutants' actual mutation descriptions (e.g., `replace += with -=`).
PR #657 merged agnostic cores for PasswordInput, SearchInput, NumberInput, PinInput — these needed mutation runs as part of the rebase catch-up.
The `ars-components` HoverCard title mount/unmount guards are idempotent (writing same boolean value), making those specific mutants structurally equivalent with no observable test surface.

code.finding: Nested `else if let Some(port_raw) = after.strip_prefix(':') { ... } else { return None; }` should be replaced with `let port_raw = after.strip_prefix(':')?;` — equivalent behavior, satisfies zero-warnings clippy policy.

HoverCard was the weakest file at 86.61% lines / 70.83% branches; the test module lacked coverage of TriggerBlur cancellation, focus/hover coordination, API event dispatch helpers, part_attrs dispatch, initial_effects branches, and close_plan shape.
Multiple-selection mode toggle on a single row produces `Set::Multiple({r1})`, NOT `Set::Single(r1)` — the representation is not normalized to Single even for cardinality-1 sets.

Equivalent-mutant skip regex patterns for DownloadTrigger scanner index-advance mutations were missing the trailing `=`, so the patterns didn't match the actual mutation descriptions reported by cargo-mutants.

workflow: Running two concurrent `cargo mutants -p ars-components` processes against the same crate produces false timeouts from cargo/test runner contention — always serialize heavy same-crate mutation runs.
Use `cargo mutants --list | wc -l` first to size the mutant set before committing to a full run; helps decide on `-j` level and whether to parallelize.
Isolated output dirs (`-o target/mutants/<name>`) are required when running multiple mutation passes so results don't overwrite each other.
For large files, `--timeout 60 -j 1` serial runs are more reliable than parallel runs when distinguishing real hangs from resource-contention timeouts.

Use `cargo test -p ars-components --lib input::module::tests::` (with `--lib`) when unit-testing new input modules to avoid hanging on the proptest integration test binary that also matches the filter.
When a mutation pass finds misses, batch all targeted tests in one patch pass rather than iterating per-mutant — addresses the full cluster at once and avoids multiple slow baseline recompilations.
Mutation timeout-only survivors (no missed, just timeout) are often equivalent boundary mutations or parser scanner loops; verify by serial rerun before documenting as equivalent.

friction: `target/coverage/` directory did not exist on first LCOV run, causing report-write failure — needed an explicit `mkdir -p target/coverage` before the coverage command.
Running two concurrent `cargo mutants -p ars-components` jobs (DownloadTrigger + Table) produced false timeouts, invalidating both runs and requiring serial reruns.
cargo-mutants `-o` flag requires the output parent directory to exist before invocation — no automatic creation.

The `.cargo/mutants.toml` skip patterns for DownloadTrigger's index-advance mutations were missing trailing `=`, so those timeout-only mutants kept appearing across reruns until the regex was corrected.

`cargo test -p ars-components input::` hung indefinitely because the filter matched both the lib unit tests and the proptest/spec-conformance integration test binaries — required `--lib` flag to isolate.
Mutation runs for large crate files (Table, DownloadTrigger, NumberInput) each take 30–60+ minutes serially, making iteration expensive.
The parallel mutation runs for NumberInput and PinInput produced timeout artifacts that required a third serial rerun to confirm they were concurrency artifacts rather than real failures.
open_items: Nightly CI run #26141232323 had 7 failing mutation shards with a total of ~185 missed mutants across components-shard-1/2/9/10/11, core-shard-2, and collections-shard-1 — the local patches should close most of these but the PR CI run is the definitive check.

The Table mutation final run ended with 0 missed mutants locally, but the earlier Nightly table shard had 61+ misses — the gap between local file-targeted runs and CI shard coverage warrants a post-merge Nightly validation.

ars-core SharedState and ars-collections Key/UUID had small survivor sets addressed by equivalent-mutant exclusions, but no additional tests were added — these could use a future test-strengthening pass if the profile-inactive code paths ever become testable.

The development docs update requires agents to run `cargo mutants` for new agnostic components, but there is no automated enforcement — a future CI gate or xtask check could formalize this requirement.
